title: Lab 1 – 2026
instructions: While you in your home directory gather detailed information about your server (taks 1 till 8) and append the output (don't overwrite) into a file named 'server_info' located in your home directory
questions: Use the appropriate command to display your present working directory.
Check kernel version of your server.
Show detailed file information (permissions, size, ownership) in the current directory.
List all files including hidden ones, with detailed information.
Create a new directory named 'Linux', then navigate into it using the cd command.
Use pwd to verify you're inside the 'Linux' directory.
While in the Linux directory, create three subdirectories: 'Redhat', 'oel', and 'debian'.
Use cd .. to go up one level, then check and confirm your present working directory using pwd.
While in your home directory, create three empty files: 'file1.txt', 'pic.jpg', and 'clip.avi'.
Create three directories in your home directory: 'documents', 'images', and 'media'. Now Move 'file1.txt' into 'documents', 'pic.jpg' into 'images', and 'clip.avi' into 'media'.
Create a nested directory structure office/hobby/personal using a single mkdir command with the appropriate option.
Copy 'file1.txt' from the 'documents' directory into the 'office' directory.
Copy 'pic.jpg' from the 'images' directory into the 'hobby' directory.
Use tree command to display the structure of the 'office' directory and store in a file named office_tree under office/hobby/personal.
